prompt: Create a vertical close-up photorealistic macro image of a vast nemophila baby blue eyes field at sunrise. The camera is placed very low among the plants, focusing on exactly 5 prominent open pale-blue flowers in the foreground: 1 tall blossom on the upper left with translucent petals, 1 open blossom near the lower center-left, 1 backlit blossom in the center, 1 smaller glowing blossom mid-right, and 1 larger open blossom on the lower right; include exactly 2 visible closed blue buds near the lower left and lower right. The petals are delicate, semi-transparent, softly veined, and covered with tiny sparkling dew droplets; thin green stems and fuzzy serrated leaves catch golden rim light. The background is a wide sea of blurred blue flowers stretching to a distant horizon, with a warm orange sun sitting low near the center-right, creating strong backlighting, lens glow, and dreamy bokeh. Use a shallow depth of field, cinematic golden-hour lighting, natural colors, gentle haze, and a serene spring atmosphere. Shot as if with a macro lens, intimate foreground detail, soft distant landscape, no people, no buildings, no text. Aspect ratio 3:4 vertical, mood peaceful and ethereal.
